description: Skills England works with partners to create better skills for better jobs, enabling growth and opportunity. Skills England is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Work and Pensions .

details.body:
Skills England works with partners to create better skills for better jobs, enabling growth and opportunity.

Skills England is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Work and Pensions.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The UK Standard Skills Classification (SSC), a new national framework, is a significant step forward in how we think about and discuss skills in the UK.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Simpler, shared system for describing skills needs launched

ordered_featured_documents.summary: A report detailing how the UK Standard Skills Classification (SSC) has been developed, and proposals for how it could be maintained.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UK Standard Skills Classification development report

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New battery manufacturing apprenticeship unit developed, following rapid consultation with employers and sector experts, to support UK’s growing battery sector.
ordered_featured_documents.title: New apprenticeship unit to supercharge battery manufacturing

ordered_featured_documents.summary: First apprentices starting on AI and automation practitioner apprenticeship. It’s open to all employers and supporting drive to upskill millions by 2030.
ordered_featured_documents.title: AI apprenticeship to close digital skills gap holding back millions of workers

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Young people will be given a quicker route into high-quality jobs on major projects as the Government slashes red tape to fast-track the process.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Britain’s growth sectors to get major skills boost from new ‘fast track’ apprenticeships reforms

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Every adult in the UK is eligible to take free, newly benchmarked courses to gain practical AI skills for work.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Free AI training for all, as government and industry programme expands to provide 10 million workers with key AI skills by 2030

ordered_featured_links.title: UK Standard Skills Classification - SSC Explorer

details.body:
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) leads the day-to-day operation of Skills England and heads the organisation’s senior leadership team.

The CEO is responsible for setting the organisation’s strategic direction, ensuring it delivers on its objectives, and ensuring it uses its powers efficiently and effectively. 

They are also responsible for building strong partnerships with key stakeholders, including government, businesses, and training providers.

role_appointments.title: Tessa Griffiths CBE - Chief Executive Officer (jobshare)

details.body:
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) leads the day-to-day operation of Skills England and heads the organisation’s senior leadership team.

The CEO is responsible for setting the organisation’s strategic direction, ensuring it delivers on its objectives, and ensuring it uses its powers efficiently and effectively. 

They are also responsible for building strong partnerships with key stakeholders, including government, businesses, and training providers.

role_appointments.title: Sarah Maclean CBE - Chief Executive Officer (jobshare)

details.body: The Deputy Chief Executive Officer supports the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) leading the day-to-day operation of Skills England.

role_appointments.title: Gemma Marsh - Deputy Chief Executive Officer

details.body: Phil is a member of the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) by virtue of his role as interim Chair of Skills England, a new arm’s length body that will bring partners together to meet the skills needs of the next 10 years. This will ensure the 2 bodies work together to form a coherent national picture of where skills gaps exist and how they can be addressed.

role_appointments.title: Phil Smith - Industrial Strategy Advisory Council member

details.body:
The Chair is responsible for leading the Board in shaping the strategic direction of Skills England and providing independent oversight, scrutiny and assurance. In collaboration with the Chief Executive Officer and senior leadership team, the Chair is responsible for:

  • shaping Skills England’s corporate strategy and key strategic objectives
  • overseeing major decisions involving the use of financial and other resources
  • having regard to Skills England’s statutory and administrative obligations

The Chair is expected to dedicate approximately 20 days per year to the role, including attendance at Board meetings, performance reviews, and engagement with stakeholders.
The Vice Chair supports the Chair in leading the Board and shaping the strategic direction.

role_appointments.title: Sir David Bell - Vice Chair, Skills England

details.body:
The board members play a vital role in guiding the strategic direction of Skills England and ensuring it delivers on its vision. As part of the Board, members provide independent insight, challenge, and support to the executive team. Working collectively with the Chair and fellow board members, they are responsible for:

  • contributing to the development and oversight of Skills England’s strategic objectives
  • providing expert advice and constructive challenge to ensure effective decision-making
  • supporting good governance, risk management, and accountability across the organisation
  • acting as ambassadors for Skills England, promoting its role and impact across the skills system

Board members are expected to dedicate approximately 20 days per year to the role, including attendance at Board meetings and engagement with stakeholders.

The board members play a vital role in guiding the strategic direction of Skills England and ensuring it delivers on its vision. As part of the Board, members provide independent insight, challenge, and support to the executive team. Working collectively with the Chair and fellow board members, they are responsible for:

  • contributing to the development and oversight of Skills England’s strategic objectives
  • providing expert advice and constructive challenge to ensure effective decision-making
  • supporting good governance, risk management, and accountability across the organisation
  • acting as ambassadors for Skills England, promoting its role and impact across the skills system

Board members are expected to dedicate approximately 20 days per year to the role, including attendance at Board meetings and engagement with stakeholders.

role_appointments.title: Dr Fazal Dad CBE - Board member

details.description: This is our general enquiries contact information. We aim to respond within 10 working days.
details.title: Contact Skills England

details.description:
Skills England FOI requests and responses are managed by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP). 

Use the DWP FOI contact form above to make an FOI request.
details.title: Make an FOI request

details.body:
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) leads the day-to-day operation of Skills England and heads the organisation’s senior leadership team.

The CEO is responsible for setting the organisation’s strategic direction, ensuring it delivers on its objectives, and ensuring it uses its powers efficiently and effectively. 

They are also responsible for building strong partnerships with key stakeholders, including government, businesses, and training providers.

The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) leads the day-to-day operation of Skills England and heads the organisation’s senior leadership team.

The CEO is responsible for setting the organisation’s strategic direction, ensuring it delivers on its objectives, and ensuring it uses its powers efficiently and effectively. 

They are also responsible for building strong partnerships with key stakeholders, including government, businesses, and training providers.
The Deputy Chief Executive Officer supports the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) leading the day-to-day operation of Skills England.

The Chair is responsible for leading the Board in shaping the strategic direction of Skills England and providing independent oversight, scrutiny and assurance. In collaboration with the Chief Executive Officer and senior leadership team, the Chair is responsible for:

  • shaping Skills England’s corporate strategy and key strategic objectives
  • overseeing major decisions involving the use of financial and other resources
  • having regard to Skills England’s statutory and administrative obligations

The Chair is expected to dedicate approximately 20 days per year to the role, including attendance at Board meetings, performance reviews, and engagement with stakeholders.
The Vice Chair supports the Chair in leading the Board and shaping the strategic direction.

The board members play a vital role in guiding the strategic direction of Skills England and ensuring it delivers on its vision. As part of the Board, members provide independent insight, challenge, and support to the executive team. Working collectively with the Chair and fellow board members, they are responsible for:

  • contributing to the development and oversight of Skills England’s strategic objectives
  • providing expert advice and constructive challenge to ensure effective decision-making
  • supporting good governance, risk management, and accountability across the organisation
  • acting as ambassadors for Skills England, promoting its role and impact across the skills system

Board members are expected to dedicate approximately 20 days per year to the role, including attendance at Board meetings and engagement with stakeholders.
